# preciousmetalscharts

> Independent live price charts for precious metals (gold, silver, platinum, palladium),
> with the gold-to-silver ratio, dealer premiums, an inflation-adjusted view, a private
> portfolio tracker and free price alerts. Independent and not affiliated with any dealer
> or mint. Educational information only — not investment advice.

## Key facts
- Metals covered: gold (XAU), silver (XAG), platinum (XPT), palladium (XPD).
- Data is referenced to global spot markets (COMEX futures and the London/LBMA market).
- Spot price = the reference price for one troy ounce; physical coins and bars cost more
  because dealers add a premium over spot.
- Gold-to-silver ratio = gold price ÷ silver price; a high ratio is read as silver being
  cheap relative to gold. Historical range: under ~35 at silver peaks to above ~100 in
  market stress.
- The site is independent: it sells no metals and holds no inventory.

## Pages
- [Charts (home)](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/): live spot prices, main chart, ratio,
  premiums, performance comparison, FAQ.
- [Gold-to-silver ratio](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/ratio): the ratio with historical
  context.
- [Premiums](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/premiums): typical dealer premium over spot
  by product.
- [Price alerts](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/alerts): free email alerts at a target price.
- [Best Gold IRA companies (comparison)](https://preciousmetalscharts.com/gold-ira):
  independent comparison of gold-IRA providers on fees, minimums, transparency and support,
  with a published scoring method and the honest downsides.

## Definitions worth quoting
- Gold IRA: a self-directed IRA holding IRS-approved physical precious metals; same tax
  treatment as a traditional or Roth IRA, with metal as the underlying asset.
- Home-storage gold IRA: not permitted — the IRS requires an approved depository.

## Contact / authority
- Publisher: preciousmetalscharts
- Editorial policy: scores follow fixed weights set before any commission terms are considered.
- Disclosure: some outbound links are affiliate links; they never influence scoring.
